# Keel

> The SQLite of durable execution: production-grade resilience (retry, backoff,
> timeout, circuit breaker, rate limit, LLM dev-cache) and opt-in crash-resumable
> durable flows, as an in-process library with zero code changes. Policy lives in
> one `keel.toml`; state is files in `.keel/`. No daemon, no port, no login.

Keel intercepts a program's outbound calls (httpx/requests/fetch, LLM SDKs,
`py:`/`ts:` function targets) and applies a policy at the call boundary. Adding
it is `keel run app.py` / `keel run app.mjs` — no imports, no context objects, no
base classes. Uninstalling the package removes the behavior and nothing else.

Status (2026-07-13): Tier 1 and Tier 2 (including async flows) work end-to-end
in both Python and Node on the real Rust core, with `kill -9` resume, a
Postgres journal backend for fleet durability, `keel record`/`keel sim`, and a
scoped Rust front end (`#[keel::wrap]` + reqwest-middleware). Wrapped-call
overhead ~0.8 µs vs a 10 µs budget. Wheels/npm/crates are not published yet —
build from source; see `docs/naming-decision.md` for the registry-name
collisions blocking that.

## Start here

- [README](README.md): status, quickstart per language, demo + conformance tables, repo layout.
- [Architecture spec](docs/architecture-spec.md): the two-tier model (Tier 1 resilience, Tier 2 durability), core, journal, front ends.
- [DX spec](docs/dx-spec.md): the "just works" ladder (Levels 0–3), DX invariants, agent-framework packs, the agent-facing surface.

## Concepts (one canonical example each)

- [Level 0 — zero config](docs/dx-spec.md): `keel run app.py` → conservative production defaults on every discovered call. Never changes success-path semantics; never retries non-idempotent calls by default.
- [Level 1 — `keel init`](docs/dx-spec.md): generate a `keel.toml` from static + observed evidence; every entry cites `file:line`. `keel init --diff` previews changes.
- [Level 2 — durable flows](demos/durable-pipeline/README.md): list `py:mod:fn` (Python) or `ts:mod#fn` (Node) under `[flows]`; a crashed run resumes and replays completed steps without re-firing effects, including concurrent async effects inside one flow.
- [Targets](contracts/policy.schema.json): `host` (e.g. `api.stripe.com`), `py:`/`ts:` functions, and semantic `llm:<provider>` / `tool:<name>` / `mcp:<server>`.
- [LLM dev-cache](demos/agent-demo/README.md): `cache = { mode = "dev" }` replays identical prompts off `KEEL_ENV=prod`; with the native core it persists across runs (~0 API calls on a repeat).

## Reference

- [Policy schema](contracts/policy.schema.json) and [smart defaults](contracts/defaults.toml).
- [Error codes](contracts/error-codes.json): the frozen `KEEL-E0NN` taxonomy. `keel explain <code>` prints what/why/next.
- [CLI](crates/keel-cli): `run · init · doctor (--effective-policy) · status · explain · flows (suggest/add/resume) · trace · replay · tail · fsck · mcp · record (run/list/test) · sim`; every command has a deterministic `--json` twin.
- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): the full corpus (all of the above expanded, with the CLI + error-code reference inline).

## For coding agents

- Run `keel doctor --json` before changing resilience behavior; propose edits as a diff (`keel init --diff`).
- `keel init --agents` drops a Keel section into `AGENTS.md`.
- `keel mcp` serves the CLI as an MCP server over stdio: tools `get_status`, `get_doctor_report`, `propose_policy` (a keel.toml diff), `get_trace`, `list_flows`, `explain_error` — each byte-identical to the matching `--json` command.
- Output is deterministic (sorted keys, no timestamps unless asked) so `--json` is diffable.
